steps.title: Identify the verb's subject
steps.content:
The blank verb is part of the relative clause “that ______ in contact with it,” which modifies nematodes.

The relative pronoun “that” refers back to “nematodes,” making “nematodes” the subject the blank verb must agree with.

Since “nematodes” is plural, the verb must take a plural form.

steps.title: Apply subject-verb agreement
steps.content:
A verb must agree with its subject in number. The plural subject “nematodes” requires the plural verb form come, while options A, B, and C are singular forms that violate this agreement.

This creates “nematodes that come in contact with it,” showing which nematodes the toxin is deadly to.

That matches choice D — D is correct.
